feature_title: Plan - Player UX and Streaming Reliability

companion_files.name: PLAN.md
companion_files.content:
# Plan - Player UX and Streaming Reliability

## Document Status

- Last updated: 2026-02-20
- Feature: Player/runtime reliability and streaming gap-fill correctness
- Scope:
  - Lyrics false-negative on first open
  - Album-level thumbs up/down batch action
  - Native cover-art missing-file self-heal
  - Volume consistency on full reload
  - Playback continuity across reload/rolling backend updates
  - Unowned album stream gap-fill not activating (TIDAL/YouTube)
  - Settings hydration consistency across browsers
- Planning maturity: `Spec Outline` complete, `Refined Spec` complete, `Detailed Implementation Plan` complete
- Implementation approval: Approved and implemented

## Chunking Protocol (Context-Safe Execution)

1. Execute one phase at a time.
2. Max two atomic tasks per implementation pass before checkpoint.
3. After each pass: run targeted tests, summarize outcomes, and commit.
4. Defer cross-cutting refactors unless required by acceptance criteria.

## Spec Outline

1. Eliminate false "No lyrics available" states when lyrics exist.
2. Add album-level thumbs up/down actions that batch-apply to tracks.
3. Heal missing native cover files immediately instead of repeatedly failing and redirecting.
4. Stabilize volume state across full page reloads.
5. Preserve play/pause intent and resume position through reloads and backend pod replacement events.
6. Ensure unowned album tracks get TIDAL/YouTube gap-fill matches when providers are available.
7. Ensure settings UI reflects persisted DB settings consistently across browser sessions.

## Refined Spec

1. Fix first-open lyrics race/caching behavior so temporary empty/fallback responses do not become long-lived false negatives.
2. Implement server-side batch preference mutation for album tracks, with frontend optimistic progress and failure reporting.
3. On native cover miss, attempt immediate download-and-store to `native:` path and persist repaired `coverUrl`.
4. Consolidate volume source-of-truth between audio state and Howler initialization.
5. Extend playback-state persistence contract to include play intent, then restore media + intent safely on hydration.
6. Remove/mitigate false-negative provider status caching and ensure TIDAL status includes lazy OAuth restore.
7. Gate settings rendering on real settings load and refresh on auth/focus to avoid default-value flash or stale display.

## Detailed Implementation Plan (Atomic Tasks)

### Phase 1: UX Correctness First

#### Task 1.1: Lyrics first-open reliability
- Status: Complete
- Acceptance criteria:
  - Opening Lyrics tab does not cache temporary empty fallback as a long-lived success state.
  - Switching tabs is no longer required to see lyrics when lyrics exist.
  - Regression tests cover first-open metadata/race path.
- Primary files:
  - `frontend/hooks/useLyrics.ts`
  - `frontend/lib/lyrics-cache-policy.ts`
  - `frontend/tests/unit/useLyrics.test.ts`
  - `frontend/components/player/OverlayPlayer.tsx`
  - `frontend/components/player/HowlerAudioElement.tsx`
  - `backend/src/routes/lyrics.ts`
  - `backend/src/routes/__tests__/lyricsRuntime.test.ts`
- Completion summary:
  - Raised route timeout ceiling and changed timeout/service-failure responses to `504`/`503` instead of returning empty-lyrics fallbacks.
  - Removed frontend fallback-to-empty on request errors and added short stale-time refresh policy for `source=none` empty lyrics payloads.
  - Added coverage for new backend timeout/error contract and lyrics stale-time policy behavior.
  - Verification:
    - `npm --prefix backend test -- --runTestsByPath src/routes/__tests__/lyricsRuntime.test.ts`
    - `npm --prefix frontend run test:unit -- tests/unit/useLyrics.test.ts`
    - `npm --prefix frontend run lint -- hooks/useLyrics.ts lib/lyrics-cache-policy.ts tests/unit/useLyrics.test.ts`

#### Task 1.2: Settings hydration consistency across browsers
- Status: Complete
- Acceptance criteria:
  - Settings page does not render default settings as final state before DB fetch resolves.
  - User/system settings reload reliably on auth-ready and focus/reconnect paths.
  - Targeted settings hook/page tests cover hydration and fallback behavior.
- Primary files:
  - `frontend/features/settings/hooks/settingsHydration.ts`
  - `frontend/features/settings/hooks/useSettingsData.ts`
  - `frontend/features/settings/hooks/useSystemSettings.ts`
  - `frontend/app/settings/page.tsx`
  - `frontend/tests/unit/settingsHydration.test.ts`
- Completion summary:
  - Added explicit settings-page loading gate so default local hook state is not rendered as final UI before API hydration completes.
  - Hardened settings hooks with failed-load retry on focus/online/visibility recovery paths using cooldown-based retry gating.
  - Added unit coverage for hydration loading gate and background retry policy helpers.
  - Verification:
    - `npm --prefix frontend run test:unit -- tests/unit/settingsHydration.test.ts tests/unit/useLyrics.test.ts`
    - `npm --prefix frontend run lint -- app/settings/page.tsx features/settings/hooks/useSettingsData.ts features/settings/hooks/useSystemSettings.ts features/settings/hooks/settingsHydration.ts tests/unit/settingsHydration.test.ts hooks/useLyrics.ts lib/lyrics-cache-policy.ts tests/unit/useLyrics.test.ts`

#### Task 1.3: Volume reload stability
- Status: Complete
- Acceptance criteria:
  - Full page reload preserves effective volume/mute state deterministically.
  - No conflicting initialization paths can overwrite intended volume on mount.
  - Targeted audio state test(s) cover reload initialization order.
- Primary files:
  - `frontend/lib/audio-volume.ts`
  - `frontend/lib/audio-state-context.tsx`
  - `frontend/lib/howler-engine.ts`
  - `frontend/components/player/HowlerAudioElement.tsx`
  - `frontend/tests/unit/audioVolume.test.ts`
- Completion summary:
  - Consolidated persisted volume parsing/clamping into shared helper utilities for deterministic reload hydration.
  - Removed independent Howler startup storage initialization so AudioState drives runtime volume/mute state as the single source of truth.
  - Added targeted unit coverage for volume clamping and persisted-volume parsing behavior.
  - Verification:
    - `npm --prefix frontend run test:unit -- tests/unit/audioVolume.test.ts tests/unit/settingsHydration.test.ts tests/unit/useLyrics.test.ts`
    - `npm --prefix frontend run lint -- app/settings/page.tsx features/settings/hooks/useSettingsData.ts features/settings/hooks/useSystemSettings.ts features/settings/hooks/settingsHydration.ts hooks/useLyrics.ts lib/lyrics-cache-policy.ts lib/audio-volume.ts lib/audio-state-context.tsx lib/howler-engine.ts components/player/HowlerAudioElement.tsx tests/unit/audioVolume.test.ts tests/unit/settingsHydration.test.ts tests/unit/useLyrics.test.ts`

### Phase 2: Stream Continuity and Provider Matching

#### Task 2.1: Unowned album gap-fill reliability (TIDAL/YouTube)
- Status: Complete
- Acceptance criteria:
  - TIDAL status endpoint can report authenticated after lazy restore (no false unauthenticated when valid DB OAuth exists).
  - Frontend negative status cache does not suppress matching for extended periods after transient failures.
  - Unowned discovery/library album tracks correctly receive `streamSource` enrichment when provider auth is valid.
  - Runtime/unit coverage added for status + gap-fill behavior.
- Primary files:
  - `backend/src/routes/tidalStreaming.ts`
  - `frontend/features/album/hooks/useTidalGapFill.ts`
  - `frontend/features/album/hooks/useYtMusicGapFill.ts`
  - `frontend/app/album/[id]/page.tsx`
- Completion summary:
  - Updated `GET /api/tidal-streaming/status` to run lazy per-user OAuth restore so sidecar restarts no longer report false unauthenticated when DB tokens are valid.
  - Added shared provider-status cache policy with short negative TTL and in-flight dedupe to reduce false-negative suppression windows while avoiding duplicate status calls.
  - Added backend runtime coverage for status-triggered restore and frontend unit coverage for positive/negative provider cache behavior.
  - Verification:
    - `npm --prefix backend test -- --runTestsByPath src/routes/__tests__/tidalStreamingRuntime.test.ts`
    - `npm --prefix frontend run test:unit -- tests/unit/providerStatusCache.test.ts`
    - `npm --prefix frontend run lint -- features/album/hooks/useTidalGapFill.ts features/album/hooks/useYtMusicGapFill.ts features/album/hooks/providerStatusCache.ts tests/unit/providerStatusCache.test.ts`

#### Task 2.2: Playback continuity through reload/rolling updates
- Status: Complete
- Acceptance criteria:
  - Persisted playback state includes play intent (`isPlaying`) in addition to media/position.
  - On reload, previously playing sessions resume playback automatically; paused sessions remain paused.
  - Resume logic is bounded and safe (no autoplay loops, no forced resume after explicit pause).
  - Backend + frontend tests cover resumed vs paused states.
- Primary files:
  - `backend/prisma/schema.prisma` (+ migration)
  - `backend/src/routes/playbackState.ts`
  - `frontend/lib/audio-playback-context.tsx`
  - `frontend/lib/audio-state-context.tsx`
  - `frontend/lib/audio-controls-context.tsx`
- Completion summary:
  - Extended `PlaybackState` persistence contract with explicit `isPlaying` intent (`backend/prisma/schema.prisma` + migration) and wired route save/get flows to carry play/pause intent safely.
  - Preserved backward compatibility by avoiding overwrite of existing persisted intent when clients omit `isPlaying` in save payloads.
  - Added startup hydration in `AudioPlaybackProvider` to restore resumed-vs-paused state from backend and persisted the play-intent flag alongside existing playback progress sync writes.
  - Added backend runtime coverage for play-intent persistence behavior and frontend unit coverage for resumed/paused hydration semantics.
  - Verification:
    - `npx prisma generate` (in `backend/`)
    - `npm --prefix backend test -- --runTestsByPath src/routes/__tests__/playbackStateRuntime.test.ts`
    - `npm --prefix backend run build`
    - `npm --prefix frontend run test:unit -- tests/unit/playbackIntent.test.ts tests/unit/providerStatusCache.test.ts`
    - `npm --prefix frontend run build`

### Phase 3: Bulk Actions and Cover-Art Self-Heal

#### Task 3.1: Album-level thumbs up/down batch
- Status: Complete
- Acceptance criteria:
  - New API supports batch applying `thumbs_up`/`thumbs_down`/`clear` across album tracks.
  - Frontend exposes album-level actions with clear progress/result feedback.
  - Batch operation uses bounded DB writes and handles partial failures safely.
  - Route/runtime coverage added for new endpoint semantics.
- Primary files:
  - `backend/src/routes/library.ts`
  - `frontend/features/album/components/AlbumActionBar.tsx`
  - `frontend/features/album/hooks/useAlbumActions.ts`
  - `frontend/lib/api.ts`
- Completion summary:
  - Added `POST /api/library/albums/:id/preference` to apply thumbs-up/down/clear in bounded batch writes across all album tracks.
  - Added album action-bar batch thumbs controls with in-flight feedback and success/failure messaging.
  - Updated frontend preference cache for album tracks immediately after successful mutation response.
  - Added runtime coverage for new batch preference endpoint semantics.
  - Verification:
    - `npm --prefix backend test -- src/routes/__tests__/libraryRuntime.test.ts`
    - `npm --prefix frontend run test:unit`

#### Task 3.2: Immediate native cover-art repair
- Status: Complete
- Acceptance criteria:
  - Native cover miss triggers immediate repair attempt that stores a local `native:` image when possible.
  - Successful repair updates album `coverUrl` and avoids repeated miss warnings for the same album.
  - Fallback path remains safe if providers fail (returns 404 without crash).
  - Runtime tests cover native miss -> repair success/failure paths.
- Primary files:
  - `backend/src/routes/library.ts`
  - `backend/src/services/imageStorage.ts`
  - `backend/src/services/deezer.ts` (if needed for fetch contract reuse)
- Completion summary:
  - Updated native cover miss flow to download/store repaired cover locally via image storage and persist `coverUrl` to `native:...` when successful.
  - Added redirect normalization so repaired covers route through local cover-art proxy path.
  - Added short-circuit branch to reuse already-healed native paths and skip repeated Deezer fetches.
  - Added runtime + compat coverage for native miss repair success and healed-path reuse behavior.
  - Verification:
    - `npm --prefix backend test -- src/routes/__tests__/libraryRuntime.test.ts src/routes/__tests__/libraryCoverArtCompat.test.ts`

### Phase 4: Verification and Drift-Proof Closeout

#### Task 4.1: Verification matrix and docs/index sync
- Status: Complete
- Acceptance criteria:
  - All touched domains have targeted tests in `docs/TEST_MATRIX.md` if commands changed.
  - `docs/FEATURE_INDEX.json` entries updated/verified for changed features.
  - `CHANGELOG.md` updated in plain English under `Unreleased`.
  - `node .github/scripts/enforce-agent-policies.mjs` and `npm run agent:preflight` pass.
- Completion summary:
  - Updated changelog entries in plain English and regenerated route map/JSDoc/domain README artifacts after feature changes.
  - Verified backend/frontend targeted tests and production builds across touched domains.
  - Ran preflight successfully after regeneration and queue updates.
